rationale: Both round-4 blockers are ADDRESSED at pinned commit 17d0b008. Reconciliation is now total over the durable unioned pending-entry set and is explicitly placed after the last blocking gate but before close on panel-present, no-panel, env-skip, config-disabled, and abandoned/Warn paths. Every uncovered entry must satisfy, verified-discharge, or refuse; GetMetadata/read and audit-write failures refuse before close, including under hatches. Discharge no longer infers resolution from Allow/Warn: it requires actual tally state showing the slot is no longer a latest-round REQUEST_CHANGES at/after the pending round, and abandoned/missing-ref/transient-Warn negative tests prevent false discharge. The union test proves old X and new B obligations both reconcile. TestPanelRefuted_HatchStillReconcilesPendingObligation covers the requested hatch matrix plus read-error refusal, and all new round-5 tests are mapped in AC11 provenance and selected by the stated verification command. No residual un-audited-merge, lost-obligation, or unreconciled hatch path remains in the planned contract.

findings.summary: The full unioned obligation set is reconciled before every close, including both gate hatches; unverifiable state and metadata errors refuse.
Discharge now requires affirmative latest-round tally evidence; bare Allow/Warn is insufficient and has named negative coverage.
Union, Warn, hatch, and GetMetadata-error scenarios are named, AC11-mapped, and command-selected.

Step 5(e) requires hatch-path tally results although step 4's sketched return tuple names only registration/applied/error; implementation must plumb or derive the Result as step 5(c) requires, otherwise safely refuse. This is editorial because the normative rule and tests are explicit.

carried_forward: round-5 APPROVE carried: mechanism confirmed TOTAL/airtight; round-6 is a precision fix for F2 discharge-evidence acquisition (re-tally from panelReg.Dir) — no design/ADR/domain/AC change
